AbstractSqlFactory.summary: This is the base class for most ISqlFactory implementations.
AbstractSqlFactory.remarks: This class creates connections, commands etc by calling the appropriate methods on a protected DbProvider instance.

CursorStatus.summary: This enum emulates the FoxPro values for the CursorAdapter.CursorStatus

DbDataColumn.summary: This class represents a DBF Field in a DbDataTable class.

DbDataRow.summary: This class represents a DBF Row in a DbDataTable class.

DbDataSource.summary: Create a DbDataSource object attached to the current workarea
DbDataSource.returns: A DbDataSource object, or NULL when the current workarea is not in use.

DbDataSource.Add.summary: This required method has not been implemented.

DbDataSource.AllowEdit.summary: TRUE when the workarea is not readonly

DbDataSource.AllowNew.summary: TRUE when the workarea is not readonly

DbDataSource.AllowRemove.summary: TRUE when the workarea is not readonly

DbDataSource.ApplySort.remarks: This method will create an index on the underlying workarea to sort the rows

DbDataSource.Clear.remarks: This method will call Zap() on the workarea, so it requires exclusive use.

DbDataSource.CopyTo.summary: This required method has not (yet) been implemented

DbDataSource.Count.summary: Returns the # of records in the RDD

doc.DbDataSource.Fields: As the meta data are the same for all records, we generate them just once.

DbDataSource.Find.summary: This required method has not (yet) been implemented

doc.DbDataSource.generateFields: Generate the meta data which will be used as source for generating the dynamic properties.

DbDataSource.GetEnumerator.summary: Returns an enumerator with which you can walk the RDD.
DbDataSource.GetEnumerator.remarks: This enumerator returns DbRecord objects for each record in the Data Source

DbDataSource.Insert.summary: This required method has not been implemented.

DbDataSource.IsReadOnly.summary: TRUE when the workarea is readonly

doc.DbDataSource.PutValue: We write through into the dbf table and signal the rest of the world, that we have changed something-

DbDataSource.RemoveIndex.summary: This required method has not (yet) been implemented

DbDataSource.SELF_index.summary: Retrieve the DbRecordObject for the record at the given position
DbDataSource.SELF_index.remarks: This property is READ ONLY. Assigning to the property will throw an exception

DbDataSource.ShowDeleted.summary: Should the Deleted Flag be included as "virtual column"

DbDataSource.ShowRecno.summary: Should the Record number be included as "virtual column"

DbDataSource_2.summary: This class implements an IBindingList on a workarea
DbDataSource_2.remarks: The class can be used TO directly bind a workarea to a data aware .Net control, such as a DataGridView Each row in the List represents a record in the workarea. Records are represented by DbRecord objects Each record

DbDataTable.summary: Create a DbDataTable object with the data from the current workarea
DbDataTable.returns: A DbDataTable object, or NULL when the current workarea is not in use.

DbDataTable.ctor.summary: Create a datatable from an IRdd object
DbDataTable.ctor.remarks: The structure of the table will be derived from the structure of the workarea. and for each row in the workarea there will be a DbDataRow added to the table You can use the normal events for the DataTable class to monitor changes. and use the GetChanges() method from the DataTable class to retrieve a subset of the table with changed rows.

DbDataTable.Save.summary: Save the changes in the DbDataTable to the underlying RDD
DbDataTable.Save.param: The RDD object for the area where the changes must be saved.

DbDataTable_2.summary: This class is used to create a System.Data.DataTable from a workarea
DbDataTable_2.remarks: The data in this table is detached from the workarea. So if you make changes to the data you will have to write that back to the workarea yourself.

DbField.summary: This class is used by the DbDataSource class to describe the fields in the workarea

DbField.ctor.summary: Initializes a new instance of the DbField class
DbField.ctor.param: Column info on which this field is based.

doc.DbFieldDescriptor.ShouldSerializeValue: Must be overwritten as the the base class declares this method as abstract.

DbRecord.summary: This class is used by the DbDataSource class to represent the records in a workarea.
DbRecord.remarks: The record class exposes the fields in the workarea as 'pseudo' properties of these fields. The DbRecord class also implements INotifyPropertychanged, so when the record is bound to a data aware control then this control will 'see' changes that were made to the record.

doc.DbRecord.ctor: the current record position, which is needed when we want to talk back to the dbf. Initializes a new instance of the DbRecord class Ordinal position in the datasource Data source to which this object belongs
doc.DbRecord.FieldChange: This method will be called if somebody changes a column value inside the dbf server. If the record number is the same as the one we're representing, the we raise the property changed event as one of our properties has changed.

DbRecord.FieldName_fieldPos.summary: Get the fieldname for a certain field position

DbRecord.FieldValue_fieldPos.summary: Get the value for a certain field position

doc.DbRecord.OnPropertyChanged: We are using this to signal the consuming components that a property has changed. As the PropertyChangedEventArgs has to provide the name of the property changed, we are usign the column name of inside the dbf server.

DbRecord.RecNo.summary: Record number in the workarea. Does not have to match the logical position in the list.

DbRecord.SELF_fieldName.summary: Read/Write fields in the workarea by name.

DbRecord.SELF_fieldPos.summary: Read/Write fields in the workarea by position.

doc.DbRecord.ToString: Always a good idea to implement this.
doc.DbRecordDescriptor.GetProperties: Same as the method before except that attributes are provided for filtering.
doc.DbRecordDescriptorProvider.GetTypeDescri: This method is being called if someone wants to reflect the type we're operating on.

ISqlFactory.summary: This interface declares the common behavior for all XSharp Data Factory classes.

ISqlFactory.AfterCommit.summary: This method is called after a transaction was committed.

ISqlFactory.AfterConnect.summary: This method is called after a connection was opened.
ISqlFactory.AfterConnect.param: The connection object that was opened.

ISqlFactory.AfterDisConnect.summary: This method is called after a connection was disconnected.

ISqlFactory.AfterOpen.summary: This method is called after a datareader was opened.
ISqlFactory.AfterOpen.param: The datareader that was created by the provider.
ISqlFactory.AfterOpen.remarks: Sometimes you may want to change the datareader before using it in the program.
ISqlFactory.AfterOpen.returns: The datareader as should be used by the SqlSelect class.

ISqlFactory.AfterRollBack.summary: This method is called after a transaction was rolled back.

ISqlFactory.BeforeCommit.summary: This method is called before a transaction is committed.

ISqlFactory.BeforeConnect.summary: This method gets called before connecting. This allows to adjust the connection string.
ISqlFactory.BeforeConnect.returns: The connection string that may be altered by the factory.

ISqlFactory.BeforeDisConnect.summary: This method is called before a connection is disconnected.

ISqlFactory.BeforeRollBack.summary: This method is called before a transaction is rolled back.

ISqlFactory.CanCreateDataSourceEnumerato.summary: Does the factory support datasource enumeration.

ISqlFactory.DriverConnect.summary: This method is called to show an interactive dialog to select a connection.
ISqlFactory.DriverConnect.param: An optional window handle that will be the owner of the dialog.
A numeric value that indicates what can be changed. For example SQL_DRIVER_PROMPT or SQL_DRIVER_COMPLETE. See the ODBC Docs for more info.
A connection string that indicates the start values for the dialog.
ISqlFactory.DriverConnect.returns: The connection string that is the result of the dialog.

ISqlFactory.EnhanceException.summary: This method is called after a transaction was committed.

ISqlFactory.GetMetaDataColumnValues.summary: Create an object array of column properties from the current datarow in the schema rowset

ISqlFactory.GetMetaDataTableValues.summary: Create an object array of table properties from the current datarow in the schema rowset

ISqlFactory.GetName.summary: Return the name of the factory.

ISqlFactory.HandleSpecialValue.summary: This method is called to translate result values for a column.
ISqlFactory.HandleSpecialValue.param: The value as returned by the dataprovider.
A setting that indicates if the server wants DateTime values as Date.
ISqlFactory.HandleSpecialValue.returns: The value that must be passed to the program.

ISqlFactory.Name.summary: Return the name of the factory.

ISqlFactory.ParameterNameInQuery.summary: Should the parameter name be included in the query. Defaults to FALSE for ODBC and OLEDB and TRUE for SQLServer

ISqlFactory.ParameterPrefix.summary: Define the character with which parameters are prefixed in queries. For example '?' (ODBC, OLEDB) or '@' (SQLServer)

ISqlFactory.QuoteChar.summary: Return the quote character for table and column names.

ISqlFactory.TranslateStatement.summary: This method is called before a statement is sent to the server.
ISqlFactory.TranslateStatement.returns: A translated version of the cStatement.

OdbcFactory.summary: This is the class that implements a Factory to access data through the Ado.Net ODBC Classes.

OleDbFactory.summary: This is the class that implements a Factory to access data through the Ado.Net Microsoft SQL Server classes.

SetSqlFactory.summary: Set a new default SQL factory.

SqlFunctions.SqlGetProp_2.param: Specifies the setting. For a list of the settings you can specify see the SQLProperty Enum.

SqlFunctions.SqlSetProp_2.param: Specifies the setting. For a list of the settings you can specify see the SQLProperty Enum.

SqlServerFactory.summary: This is the class that implements a Factory to access data through the Ado.Net Microsoft SQL Server classes.

doc.SQLStatement.ParseCommand: Foxpro detect the parameter with the quotation character '?' if the character followed is '@', It is an output parameter if the character followed is '(', It is an VFP expression closed between parenthesis If the parameter are variables, they need to be private or publics and initialized before

SqlWhereType.summary: This enum emulates the FoxPro defines for the CursorAdapter.WhereType property

VFPErrorMessage.summary:
List of error codes used in the XSharp VTP support.
Each error code represents a translatable string in the string table inside XSharp.Core.

XTokenList.summary: The XSharpTokenList class. This class is used to process a list of tokens without having to worry about the end of the list. You can safely access next items through the La.. and Lt.. properties and methods.
